title: Perf: RunPod post-install model VERIFICATION takes longer than the download itself
description: OBSERVED on a live RunPod Pod (2026-07-22, portable 1.1.0 install test). After a model pack finishes downloading to the Pod, the app runs a post-install VERIFY step (the Model Library tile sits on 'Verifying...' after the download bar completes). That verify phase takes LONGER than the actual download of the model - a ridiculous ratio. Downloading is the expensive-looking part; verification should be near-instant, not the dominant cost. Likely a per-file re-hash (SHA256) of every weight AFTER it lands, run serially and/or over the network volume, instead of verifying while streaming (we already hash-while-streaming locally per MPI-296) or trusting the size/manifest. NEXT: find the remote verify path (wrapper.py on the Pod, or remoteModels.js / remoteEngine.js app-side) and measure where the time goes; check whether it re-reads every byte off the network volume to hash it. FIX DIRECTION (unverified): verify during the stream like the local path, or verify by size + expected-hash manifest without a full re-read, or parallelise. Adjacent to MPI-329 (cold model-load ~2min) but DISTINCT - that is load-into-VRAM at gen time; this is install-time verification. Top-of-todo priority (user, 2026-07-22).
